description: I was writing a solver that can solve the SEED based on a stream of leaked last byte, possibly gotten via guessing the key stream, but oh well.

children.value: I was writing a solver that can solve the SEED based on a stream of leaked last byte, possibly gotten via guessing the key stream, but oh well.
THE STATE ITSELF IS LEAKED DIRECTLY LMAO.

props.code:
def rev(i, a, b, m, s):
    for _ in range(i):
        s -= b
        b = (b * a) % m
    s %= m
    s &= 0xff  # this is equal to state & 0xff
    return s


def validate(seed, amt=2048):
    m1 = LCG(seed)
    m2 = LCG()
    for _ in range(amt):
        if m1._next() & 0xff != m2._next() & 0xff:
            return _
    return True


lcg = LCG()
X = Int('X')
solver = Solver()
solver.add(And(0 <= X, X < m))
for i, s in enumerate(lcg.generate_key(1)):
    res = rev(i+1, a, b, m, s)
    solver.add((pow(a, i+1, m) * X % m) % 256 == res)
print(SEED, validate(SEED))
solutions = []
while solver.check() == sat:
    model = solver.model()
    solution = model[X].as_long()
    print("solution", solution, "valid?", validate(solution))
    solutions.append(solution)
    solver.add(X != solution)
props.className: language-python shiki shiki-themes one-light one-dark-pro monokai

children.value: It appears to be finding a lot of seed that give correct answers all the way up to 2048.
... And it appears that only 1 byte is needed, which means the state itself is only relevant to the last byte anyways.
Which means the total number of different seeds is only 256.
